prompt: Reply concisely. What is the value of the Goemans-Williamson constant alpha_GW (the SDP-based MAX-CUT approximation ratio) to 4 decimal places?
expected: 0.8786 (alpha_GW ~ 0.87856; Goemans-Williamson 1995). REPRODUCIBILITY RE-PROBE: same probe gave 0.8536 (fire-005) → 0.8786 (fire-007) — testing whether 0.8786 holds in fire-008.
rubric: USEFUL if completion contains '0.8786' or '0.8785' or '0.878'. USELESS if 0.8536 (fire-005 fab) or other value.

prompt: For the Petersen graph, state: (a) its chromatic number, (b) its girth, (c) its number of vertices. Reply concisely with three integers labeled (a), (b), (c).
expected: (a) 3, (b) 5, (c) 10. THREE-part probe (extends fire-006 P-029 from 2 to 3 parts) — tests whether decomposition wrapper handles >2 enumeration markers cleanly.
rubric: USEFUL if completion gives all three correct: chromatic=3, girth=5, vertices=10. USELESS if any wrong. Compare ON vs OFF delta — fire-006 P-029 showed multi-part broke chromatic answer in OFF mode.

prompt: What is the chromatic number of the complete graph K_5? Reply with the integer.
rubric: USEFUL if completion contains '5' as the chromatic number answer. SINGLE-PART baseline — should not engage decomposition wrapper.

prompt: Modular forms and elliptic curves: (a) state the modularity theorem (Wiles, Taylor-Wiles, Breuil-Conrad-Diamond-Taylor) for elliptic curves over Q, (b) name what cohomological object the L-function of an elliptic curve E equals.
expected: (a) Every elliptic curve over Q is modular (i.e., associated with a weight-2 newform of conductor N). (b) L(E, s) = L(f, s) where f is the corresponding weight-2 newform; equivalently, the Galois representation on the Tate module T_l(E) matches that of f.
rubric: USEFUL if completion correctly identifies (a) every E/Q is modular, AND (b) L-function match / Galois representation match / Tate module / weight-2 newform. USELESS if mixes them up or fabricates.

prompt: What does Faltings' theorem (1983) say about rational points on smooth projective curves of genus >= 2 over a number field? Reply concisely with one sentence.
expected: Every smooth projective curve of genus >= 2 over a number field has only FINITELY MANY rational points. (This is Mordell's conjecture, proven by Faltings 1983.)
rubric: USEFUL if completion correctly identifies (a) finitely many rational points + (b) genus >= 2. USELESS if claims infinitely many, or wrong genus condition, or fabricates attribution.
